text.source_text: इत श्चा क्षिस्थानः पुरुषः परमेश्वरः , यस्माच्छ्रुतोपनिषत्कस्य श्रुतरहस्यविज्ञानस्य ब्रह्मविदो या गतिर्देवयानाख्या प्रसिद्धा श्रुतौ — ‘ अ थो त्तरेण तपसा ब्रह्मचर्येण श्रद्धया विद्ययात्मानमन्विष्यादित्यमभिजयन्ते । एतद्वै प्राणानामायतनमेतदमृतमभयमेतत्परायणमेतस्मान्न पुनरावर्तन्ते ’ (प्र. उ. १ । १०) इति , स्मृतावपि — ‘ अग्निर्ज्योतिरहः शुक्लः षण्मासा उत्तरायणम् । तत्र प्रयाता गच्छन्ति ब्रह्म ब्रह्मविदो जनाः ’ (भ. गी. ८ । २४) इति , सैवेहाक्षिपुरुषविदोऽभिधीयमाना दृश्यते । ‘ अथ यदु चैवास्मिञ्छव्यं कुर्वन्ति यदि च नार्चिषमेवाभिसम्भवन्ति ’ इत्युपक्रम्य ‘ आदित्याच्चन्द्रमसं चन्द्रमसो विद्युतं तत्पुरुषोऽमानवः स एनान्ब्रह्म गमयत्येष देवपथो ब्रह्मपथ एतेन प्रतिपद्यमाना इमं मानवमावर्तं नावर्तन्ते ’ (छा. उ. ४ । १५ । ५) इति । तदिह ब्रह्मविद्विषयया प्रसिद्धया गत्या अक्षिस्थानस्य ब्रह्मत्वं निश्चीयते ॥ १६ ॥
text.transliteration_iast: ita ścā kṣisthānaḥ puruṣaḥ parameśvaraḥ , yasmācchrutopaniṣatkasya śrutarahasyavijñānasya brahmavido yā gatirdevayānākhyā prasiddhā śrutau — ‘ a tho ttareṇa tapasā brahmacaryeṇa śraddhayā vidyayātmānamanviṣyādityamabhijayante | etadvai prāṇānāmāyatanametadamṛtamabhayametatparāyaṇametasmānna punarāvartante ’ (pra. u. 1 | 10) iti , smṛtāvapi — ‘ agnirjyotirahaḥ śuklaḥ ṣaṇmāsā uttarāyaṇam | tatra prayātā gacchanti brahma brahmavido janāḥ ’ (bha. gī. 8 | 24) iti , saivehākṣipuruṣavido'bhidhīyamānā dṛśyate | ‘ atha yadu caivāsmiñchavyaṃ kurvanti yadi ca nārciṣamevābhisambhavanti ’ ityupakramya ‘ ādityāccandramasaṃ candramaso vidyutaṃ tatpuruṣo'mānavaḥ sa enānbrahma gamayatyeṣa devapatho brahmapatha etena pratipadyamānā imaṃ mānavamāvartaṃ nāvartante ’ (chā. u. 4 | 15 | 5) iti | tadiha brahmavidviṣayayā prasiddhayā gatyā akṣisthānasya brahmatvaṃ niścīyate || 16 ||

translation.literal_translation: And for this reason too, the person in the eye is the Supreme Lord: the devayana path known in shruti as belonging to the knower of Brahman and one who has received the hidden teaching, and also known in smriti, is seen here as being taught for the knower of the eye-person. Beginning with mention of the archis path and continuing through sun, moon, lightning, and the non-human guide to Brahman, the text says those who proceed by this divine path do not return to the human cycle. Hence, by this well-known path associated with knowers of Brahman, the Brahmanhood of the eye-located person is decisively established. (16)
translation.technical_translation: Bhashya on 1.2.16 uses gati-lakshana: the arciradi/devayana sequence tied to brahmavid in canonical sources confirms akshi-purusha as Brahman.
translation.interpretive_note: Soteriological destination evidence determines identity of referent.

lexical_notes.contextual_sense: Devayana trajectory serves as inferential mark that the eye-person is Brahman, object of brahmavid travel.
Canonical sequence tied to Brahmavid destination, reinforcing high-level referent identity.

lexical_notes.gloss: movement of the knower of Brahman
lexical_notes.contextual_sense: Path references are interpreted as pertaining to Brahman-realization context, not lower deity attainment.

lexical_notes.gloss: scriptural esoteric teaching heard in tradition
lexical_notes.contextual_sense: Invoked to anchor the path doctrine in recognized Upanishadic authority chains.
Conclusion strengthened by correlating eye-person teaching with devayana destination logic.

uncertainty_notes: Curated in contiguous S02 pass with stable cadence and inferential-tag schema.
A later philological pass should revisit antaryamin technical vocabulary and akshi-purusha predicate nuances in records 1.2.040-1.2.046.
